pathwayTagline: Protect → observe → move → restore → clean → feed → eliminate

skills.title: Hand Hygiene (Hand Washing)
skills.studentFocus: First skill on many CA exam lists; required before all patient contact.

steps.detailedTagText: Introduce yourself and identify the patient.
steps.note: When: Skills where the wrong identity = real harm (feeding S10, catheter S17, bed bath S11). How: "Hi, I'm [name], your nursing assistant. May I check your ID band?"

steps.detailedTagText: Turn on the water and adjust the flow so that the water is warm.
Wet hands thoroughly, keeping hands and forearms lower than elbows. Avoid splashing water on uniform.
steps.note: Avoid splashing on uniform; keep forearms and wrists lower than elbows.

steps.detailedTagText: Apply a palm-sized amount of hand soap.
Perform hand hygiene using plenty of lather and friction for at least 20 seconds.
subSteps: Rub hands palm to palm
Rub back of right and left hand (fingers interlaced)
Rub palm to palm with fingers interlaced
Perform rotational rubbing of left and right thumbs
Rub fingertips against palm of opposite hand
Rub wrists
Repeat sequence at least two times
steps.examScorecard: Technique: Lather all surfaces ≥20 sec; repeat full sequence ≥2×

steps.detailedTagText: Keep fingertips pointing downward throughout the entire handwashing process.
steps.note: Credentia absorbs this into Steps 5 & 7 — GWC scores it as a standalone observable action. Students must demonstrate it explicitly.
steps.examScorecard: Technique: Fingertips down throughout entire wash

steps.detailedTagText: Clean under fingernails.
subSteps: Rub fingertips against palm of opposite hand
steps.examScorecard: Technique: Clean under all fingernails

steps.detailedTagText: Rinse hands with water, keeping fingertips pointing down so water runs off fingertips. Do not shake water from your hands.
steps.examScorecard: Technique: Rinse fingertips-down; do not shake water from hands

steps.detailedTagText: Dry hands thoroughly from fingers to wrists with a paper towel or air dryer. Dispose of the paper towel(s).
steps.examScorecard: Technique: Dry fingertip-to-wrist; use paper towel or air dryer

steps.detailedTagText: Use a new paper towel to turn off the water. Dispose of the paper towel.
steps.note: GWC: paper towel only. Credentia also allows knee/foot control — GWC governs on exam.
steps.examScorecard: Technique: Turn off faucet with clean paper towel; dispose immediately

steps.text: Never lean on or touch sink
steps.rawText: Never lean on or touch sink
steps.detailedTagText: Do not lean against the sink or touch the inside of the sink during the handwashing process.
steps.examScorecard: Safety: No contact with sink at any time (lean or touch)

skills.title: Donning and Removing PPE (Gown and Gloves)
skills.studentFocus: Isolation skill — no patient intro; gloves off before gown at exit.

steps.detailedTagText: Face the back opening of the gown.
Unfold the gown.
Put arms into the sleeves.
Secure the neck opening at the back of neck.
Secure the waist, making sure that the back flaps overlap each other and cover clothing as completely as possible.
Don clean, non-sterile gloves.
steps.note: Use clean, non-sterile gloves for isolation care.

steps.detailedTagText: Ensure the gloves overlap the gown sleeves at the wrist.
steps.examScorecard: Technique: Gloves overlap gown cuffs at wrist

steps.detailedTagText: When the care is complete and before leaving the room, remove the gloves BEFORE removing the gown.
steps.note: Sequence matters: gloves off before gown, before leaving the room — evaluator watches for this order.
steps.examScorecard: Safety: Remove gloves BEFORE gown; BEFORE leaving room

steps.detailedTagText: Remove the gloves, turning them inside out.
steps.note: S02 cue: "Dirty to dirty, clean to clean." Never touch the outer surface with bare skin. Order-dependent — cannot be corrected if done wrong REGIONAL-TESTING-CENTER.pdf.
subSteps: Grasp outside of first glove at palm; peel off
Slide fingers of bare hand under cuff of remaining glove at wrist; peel off turning inside out
steps.examScorecard: Technique: Both gloves removed inside out

steps.detailedTagText: Dispose of the gloves in the appropriate container.
steps.examScorecard: Safety: Dispose without touching outer glove surface

steps.detailedTagText: Perform hand hygiene.
steps.note: ⚠️ VIDEO WARNING: This step is missing from the reference video — video performs hand hygiene only after gown disposal. GWC requires it HERE, between glove removal and gown unfastening.
steps.examScorecard: Safety: Hand hygiene between gloves-off and gown-off

steps.detailedTagText: Unfasten the gown at the neck.
steps.examScorecard: Technique: Unfasten neck before removing gown

steps.detailedTagText: Unfasten the gown at the waist.
steps.examScorecard: Technique: Unfasten waist separately from neck

steps.detailedTagText: Remove the gown starting at the top of the shoulders, turning it inside out and folding the soiled area to soiled area.
subSteps: Start at shoulders; pull down
Turn inside out as you remove
Fold soiled area to soiled area
Hold away from body; do not touch floor
steps.examScorecard: Technique: Remove from shoulders; inside out; soiled-to-soiled

steps.detailedTagText: Dispose of the gown in an appropriate container.
steps.examScorecard: Safety: Dispose without contaminating self

steps.detailedTagText: Perform hand hygiene.
steps.note: Verbalize: "At this time I would wash my hands." Michelle's exact phrase in S07, S11, S12 ALL-TRANSCRIPTS-S01-S22.md. Washing before documenting satisfies GWC's before-recording requirement.
steps.examScorecard: Safety: Final hand hygiene after full PPE removal

skills.title: Counts and Records Radial Pulse
skills.studentFocus: Full 60 seconds at radial artery; document ±4.

steps.detailedTagText: Introduce yourself by name and title; explain the procedure clearly, slowly, and directly, maintaining face-to-face contact.
steps.note: Respirations only: Say "I'll be timing your breathing" — never say "counting"; awareness changes the rate. On exam day: Introduce once at the start; GWC does not require re-introduction for each skill REGIONAL-TESTING-CENTER.pdf.

steps.detailedTagText: Place the tips of three fingers on the thumb side of the patient's wrist.
Count beats for a full 60 seconds.
steps.note: Start on a whole number on the watch, then count

steps.detailedTagText: Place the call light (or signaling device) within reach and confirm the patient can use it.
steps.note: Exam rule: GWC states call signal must always be within reach when leaving REGIONAL-TESTING-CENTER.pdf. Order confirmed by transcripts: CALL_LIGHT → BED_LOW → HAND_HYGIENE (S03, S05) ALL-TRANSCRIPTS-S01-S22.md. Say: "Here's your call light — let me know if you need anything."

steps.detailedTagText: Perform hand hygiene (20 sec soap and water, or alcohol rub if hands not visibly soiled).
steps.note: Wash before documenting

steps.detailedTagText: Record the pulse rate accurately.
steps.examScorecard: Tolerance: ±4 beats/min vs evaluator

skills.title: Counts and Records Respirations
skills.studentFocus: Do not tell the patient you are counting respirations.

steps.detailedTagText: Introduce yourself by name and title; explain the procedure clearly, slowly, and directly, maintaining face-to-face contact.
steps.note: Say "for testing purposes." In real practice, never tell the patient you're counting respirations — it changes the rate.

steps.detailedTagText: Observe the rise and fall of the chest for a full 60 seconds.
steps.note: Start on a whole number on the watch, then count.

steps.detailedTagText: Place the call light (or signaling device) within reach and confirm the patient can use it.
steps.note: Exam rule: GWC states call signal must always be within reach when leaving REGIONAL-TESTING-CENTER.pdf. Order confirmed by transcripts: CALL_LIGHT → BED_LOW → HAND_HYGIENE (S03, S05) ALL-TRANSCRIPTS-S01-S22.md. Say: "Here's your call light — let me know if you need anything."

steps.detailedTagText: Perform hand hygiene (20 sec soap and water, or alcohol rub if hands not visibly soiled).
steps.note: Wash before documenting.

steps.detailedTagText: Record the respiratory rate accurately.
steps.examScorecard: Tolerance: ±4 breaths/min vs evaluator

skills.title: Measures and Records Manual Blood Pressure
skills.studentFocus: Cuff on bare skin; cleanse equipment; document ±8 mmHg.

steps.detailedTagText: Introduce yourself by name and title, explain the procedure to the patient in clear, plain language, and obtain verbal consent before proceeding.
steps.note: Respirations only: Say "I'll be timing your breathing" — never say "counting"; awareness changes the rate. On exam day: Introduce once at the start; GWC does not require re-introduction for each skill REGIONAL-TESTING-CENTER.pdf.

steps.detailedTagText: Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.
steps.note: Always physically demonstrate the first wash of your exam session — this is your only full demo. GWC Key Step = automatic fail if skipped REGIONAL-TESTING-CENTER.pdf. Phrase: "Give me a moment — I'm going to wash my hands."

steps.detailedTagText: Cleanse the stethoscope and blood pressure cuff prior to placing it on the patient's skin.

steps.text: Position patient; arm at heart level
steps.rawText: Position patient; arm at heart level
steps.detailedTagText: Place the patient in a relaxed reclining or sitting position. The patient should be seated quietly for at least five minutes in a chair prior to blood pressure measurement. Ask the patient which arm they prefer to use. Both feet should be on the floor and the arm should be supported at heart level.
steps.note: Ask which arm the patient prefers.

steps.detailedTagText: Remove or rearrange clothing so the cuff and the stethoscope are on bare skin.

Center the bladder of the blood pressure cuff over the brachial artery with the lower margin 1″ above the antecubital space. Fit the cuff evenly and snugly. Palpate the brachial artery in the antecubital space.
steps.note: Lower cuff margin sits 1″ above the elbow bend.

steps.detailedTagText: Inflate the cuff to 160-180 mmHg.
steps.note: If a beat is heard immediately on deflation, fully deflate, wait 1 min, reinflate to no more than 200.
steps.examScorecard: Technique: Inflate cuff 160–180 mmHg

steps.detailedTagText: Deflate the cuff gradually at a constant rate by opening the valve on the bulb (2-3 mm Hg/second) until the first Korotkoff sound is heard. Note the systolic pressure.
steps.examScorecard: Technique: Deflate rate 2–3 mm Hg/second (systolic)

steps.detailedTagText: Continue to deflate the cuff slowly at 2 mm Hg/second. Note the point at which Korotkoff sounds disappear completely as the diastolic pressure.
steps.examScorecard: Technique: Deflate rate 2 mm Hg/second (diastolic)

steps.detailedTagText: Deflate the cuff completely and remove the cuff from the patient's arm.
Inform the patient of the blood pressure reading.
Cleanse the stethoscope and blood pressure cuff.
Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.
steps.note: Verbalize: "At this time I would wash my hands." Michelle's exact phrase in S07, S11, S12 ALL-TRANSCRIPTS-S01-S22.md. Washing before documenting satisfies GWC's before-recording requirement.

steps.detailedTagText: Document both systolic and diastolic pressures each within plus or minus 8 mmHg of the evaluator's reading.
steps.note: Both numbers must each be within ±8 mmHg.
steps.examScorecard: Exam tolerance: Document BP ±8 mmHg (systolic AND diastolic)

skills.title: Measures and Records Weight of Ambulatory Client
skills.studentFocus: Zero scale; nonskid footwear; document ±2 lbs.

steps.detailedTagText: Introduce yourself by name and title, explain the procedure to the patient in clear, plain language, and obtain verbal consent before proceeding.
steps.note: Respirations only: Say "I'll be timing your breathing" — never say "counting"; awareness changes the rate. On exam day: Introduce once at the start; GWC does not require re-introduction for each skill REGIONAL-TESTING-CENTER.pdf.

steps.detailedTagText: Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.
steps.note: Always physically demonstrate the first wash of your exam session — this is your only full demo. GWC Key Step = automatic fail if skipped REGIONAL-TESTING-CENTER.pdf. Phrase: "Give me a moment — I'm going to wash my hands."

steps.detailedTagText: Verify the patient is wearing nonskid footwear.
Balance (or zero) scale.
Walk the patient to the scale.
Assist the patient to step on the scale.
Check that the patient is centered on the scale.
Check that the patient has their arms at their side.
Ensure the patient is not holding on to anything that would alter the reading of the weight.
Adjust the weights until the scale is in balance or read analog scale.
Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.
steps.note: Verbalize: "At this time I would wash my hands." Michelle's exact phrase in S07, S11, S12 ALL-TRANSCRIPTS-S01-S22.md. Washing before documenting satisfies GWC's before-recording requirement.

steps.detailedTagText: Document weight (plus/minus 2 lbs. or 0.9 kg).
steps.note: Tolerance: record weight within ±2 lb / 0.9 kg of actual.
steps.examScorecard: Exam tolerance: Document weight ±2 lb / 0.9 kg

steps.detailedTagText: Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.
steps.note: Verbalize: "At this time I would wash my hands." Michelle's exact phrase in S07, S11, S12 ALL-TRANSCRIPTS-S01-S22.md. Washing before documenting satisfies GWC's before-recording requirement.

skills.title: Measures and Records Urinary Output
skills.studentFocus: Measure in container — not the same as full bedpan skill.

steps.detailedTagText: Introduce yourself by name and title, explain the procedure to the patient in clear, plain language, and obtain verbal consent before proceeding.
steps.note: Respirations only: Say "I'll be timing your breathing" — never say "counting"; awareness changes the rate. On exam day: Introduce once at the start; GWC does not require re-introduction for each skill REGIONAL-TESTING-CENTER.pdf.

steps.detailedTagText: Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.
steps.note: Always physically demonstrate the first wash of your exam session — this is your only full demo. GWC Key Step = automatic fail if skipped REGIONAL-TESTING-CENTER.pdf. Phrase: "Give me a moment — I'm going to wash my hands."

steps.detailedTagText: Don clean, non-sterile gloves.
steps.note: GWC rule: Gloves required before any contact with body fluids, mucous membranes, or non-intact skin. Failure to glove = no credit; cannot be corrected mid-skill REGIONAL-TESTING-CENTER.pdf. In PPE (S02): pull cuff over gown sleeve to cover skin.

steps.detailedTagText: Pour the liquid in the bedpan into a measuring container.
Rinse the bedpan and empty the water into the toilet.
Measure the amount of urine at eye level with the container on a flat surface.
Empty the urine into the toilet.
Rinse the measuring container with water and empty it into the toilet.

Remove gloves by turning them inside out — grasp the outside of one glove at the wrist and peel it off, then slide the fingers of the ungloved hand under the remaining glove at the wrist and peel it off turning inside out. Dispose of both gloves in the appropriate waste container without touching the outer surface.
steps.note: S02 cue: "Dirty to dirty, clean to clean." Never touch the outer surface with bare skin. Order-dependent — cannot be corrected if done wrong REGIONAL-TESTING-CENTER.pdf.
subSteps: Grasp outside of first glove at wrist, peel off
bare fingers slide under cuff of second glove, peel off inside-out

steps.detailedTagText: Record the volume within plus or minus 25 mL of the actual volume.
steps.note: Tolerance: record urine volume within ±25 mL of actual.
steps.examScorecard: Exam tolerance: Record volume ±25 mL

steps.detailedTagText: Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.
steps.note: Verbalize: "At this time I would wash my hands." Michelle's exact phrase in S07, S11, S12 ALL-TRANSCRIPTS-S01-S22.md. Washing before documenting satisfies GWC's before-recording requirement.

skills.title: Positions on Side
skills.studentFocus: Pillows for alignment; face not against side rail.

steps.detailedTagText: Introduce yourself by name and title, explain the procedure to the patient in clear, plain language, and obtain verbal consent before proceeding.
steps.note: Respirations only: Say "I'll be timing your breathing" — never say "counting"; awareness changes the rate. On exam day: Introduce once at the start; GWC does not require re-introduction for each skill REGIONAL-TESTING-CENTER.pdf.

steps.detailedTagText: Provide for patient privacy (close the curtain, door, or privacy screen).
steps.note: Do it: any time the body is exposed (bath, PROM, transfers, peri-care, dressing, stocking). Skip it: pulse, BP, weight, urinary output, hand hygiene, dentures — no body exposure ALL-TRANSCRIPTS-S01-S22.md.

steps.detailedTagText: Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.
steps.note: Always physically demonstrate the first wash of your exam session — this is your only full demo. GWC Key Step = automatic fail if skipped REGIONAL-TESTING-CENTER.pdf. Phrase: "Give me a moment — I'm going to wash my hands."

steps.detailedTagText: Position the bed flat.
Raise the bed height.

steps.text: Raise far side rail for safety
steps.rawText: Raise far side rail for safety
steps.detailedTagText: Raise the side rail on the side of the bed the patient will be facing after repositioning for safety.

steps.text: Move to working side of bed
steps.rawText: Move to working side of bed
steps.detailedTagText: Move to the working side of the bed, which is opposite the side rail that was raised.

steps.text: Explain move toward you on three
steps.rawText: Explain move toward you on three
steps.detailedTagText: Explain to the patient that the candidate will move them closer to the candidate before turning on the count of three.

steps.text: Count three; move patient toward you
steps.rawText: Count three; move patient toward you
steps.detailedTagText: From the working side of the bed count to three and move the patient towards you.

steps.text: Instruct patient to move near arm
steps.rawText: Instruct patient to move near arm
steps.detailedTagText: Instruct the patient to move their arm closest to the raised side rail away from their body. If able, the patient should grasp the side rail with the hand closest to you, reaching across their own body.

steps.text: Raise patient's near knee to assist
steps.rawText: Raise patient's near knee to assist
steps.detailedTagText: Raise the patient's knee that is closest to the candidate to assist in turning.

steps.text: Explain turning toward rail on three
steps.rawText: Explain turning toward rail on three
steps.detailedTagText: Explain that the candidate will turn the patient towards the side rail on count three.

steps.text: Count three; turn toward side rail
steps.rawText: Count three; turn toward side rail
steps.detailedTagText: Count to three to turn the patient towards the raised side rail.

steps.text: Guard face from rail and pillow
steps.rawText: Guard face from rail and pillow
steps.detailedTagText: Ensure that the patient's face never comes close to the side rail or becomes covered by the pillow.

steps.text: Check patient not on bottom arm
steps.rawText: Check patient not on bottom arm
steps.detailedTagText: Check that the patient is not lying on their bottom arm.
Place a pillow behind the patient's back, ensuring they will not roll back to the supine position.

steps.text: Check body alignment from bed end
steps.rawText: Check body alignment from bed end
steps.detailedTagText: Move to the end of the bed and check that the patient is in correct body alignment.
Verify that the patient is in the middle of the bed.

steps.text: Pillow under top arm; protect ribs
steps.rawText: Pillow under top arm; protect ribs
steps.detailedTagText: Place a pillow between the patient's top arm and their rib cage or the bed, ensuring the elbow is not directly on their ribs.

steps.text: Pillow under top knee; protect ankles
steps.rawText: Pillow under top knee; protect ankles
steps.detailedTagText: Place a pillow under the top knee, ensuring the knee is not resting directly on the other knee or the ankle is not on top of the other ankle.
Adjust the pillow under the patient's head for comfort.
Lower the bed to its lowest position and verify the bed brakes are locked.
steps.note: Two actions, one step: lower AND lock. Physically press the brake and confirm resistance — evaluator watches for the physical check REGIONAL-TESTING-CENTER.pdf. S03 transcript: "make sure the bed is in low position" before washing ALL-TRANSCRIPTS-S01-S22.md.

steps.detailedTagText: Place the call light (or signaling device) within reach of the patient and confirm the patient knows how to use it.
steps.note: Exam rule: GWC states call signal must always be within reach when leaving REGIONAL-TESTING-CENTER.pdf. Order confirmed by transcripts: CALL_LIGHT → BED_LOW → HAND_HYGIENE (S03, S05) ALL-TRANSCRIPTS-S01-S22.md. Say: "Here's your call light — let me know if you need anything."

steps.detailedTagText: Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.
steps.note: Verbalize: "At this time I would wash my hands." Michelle's exact phrase in S07, S11, S12 ALL-TRANSCRIPTS-S01-S22.md. Washing before documenting satisfies GWC's before-recording requirement.

skills.title: Transfers from Bed to Wheelchair Using Transfer Belt
skills.studentFocus: Lock bed and wheelchair brakes; ask dizzy; pivot.

steps.detailedTagText: Introduce yourself by name and title, explain the procedure to the patient in clear, plain language, and obtain verbal consent before proceeding.
steps.note: Respirations only: Say "I'll be timing your breathing" — never say "counting"; awareness changes the rate. On exam day: Introduce once at the start; GWC does not require re-introduction for each skill REGIONAL-TESTING-CENTER.pdf.

steps.note: Do it: any time the body is exposed (bath, PROM, transfers, peri-care, dressing, stocking). Skip it: pulse, BP, weight, urinary output, hand hygiene, dentures — no body exposure ALL-TRANSCRIPTS-S01-S22.md.

steps.detailedTagText: Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.
steps.note: Always physically demonstrate the first wash of your exam session — this is your only full demo. GWC Key Step = automatic fail if skipped REGIONAL-TESTING-CENTER.pdf. Phrase: "Give me a moment — I'm going to wash my hands."

steps.detailedTagText: Check the brakes on the bed to ensure they are locked.

steps.text: Remove wheelchair foot pedals if needed
steps.rawText: Remove wheelchair foot pedals if needed
steps.detailedTagText: Remove the foot pedals from the wheelchair if needed.

steps.text: Assist patient to sit; dangle feet
steps.rawText: Assist patient to sit; dangle feet
steps.detailedTagText: Assist the patient to a seated position on the side of the bed with their feet on the floor; allow them to dangle their feet for a few minutes.
Assist the patient in putting on nonskid footwear.
Place the gait belt on the patient.

steps.text: Position wheelchair; strong side toward it
steps.rawText: Position wheelchair; strong side toward it
steps.detailedTagText: Position the wheelchair at the head or foot of the bed so the patient will move towards the wheelchair with the stronger side of their body. The wheelchair should touch the side of the bed.
Lock the brakes on the wheelchair.
Ask the patient if they feel dizzy or light-headed.
Face the patient and place each of feet in front of the patient's feet to prevent them from slipping.
Instruct the patient to push up on the bed to aid in standing on the count of three.
Grasp the gait belt with both hands, with palms and fingertips pointing up.

steps.text: Count three; assist patient to stand
steps.rawText: Count three; assist patient to stand
steps.detailedTagText: Count to three and assist the patient to stand.
Assist the patient to pivot.

steps.text: Instruct patient to grasp wheelchair arms
steps.rawText: Instruct patient to grasp wheelchair arms
steps.detailedTagText: Instruct the patient to grasp the arms of the wheelchair when they can feel the back of their knees are in contact with the wheelchair seat.

steps.text: Assist patient to seated in wheelchair
steps.rawText: Assist patient to seated in wheelchair
steps.detailedTagText: Assist the patient to a seated position in the wheelchair.
Remove the gait belt gently to avoid skin injury.
Release the wheelchair brakes.
Place the call light (or signaling device) within reach of the patient and confirm the patient knows how to use it.
steps.note: Exam rule: GWC states call signal must always be within reach when leaving REGIONAL-TESTING-CENTER.pdf. Order confirmed by transcripts: CALL_LIGHT → BED_LOW → HAND_HYGIENE (S03, S05) ALL-TRANSCRIPTS-S01-S22.md. Say: "Here's your call light — let me know if you need anything."

steps.detailedTagText: Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.
steps.note: Verbalize: "At this time I would wash my hands." Michelle's exact phrase in S07, S11, S12 ALL-TRANSCRIPTS-S01-S22.md. Washing before documenting satisfies GWC's before-recording requirement.

steps.detailedTagText: Place nonskid footwear on the patient.
Adjust the bed to a safe level.
Check the brakes of the bed to ensure they are locked.
Allow the patient to sit and dangle on the edge of the bed before standing to ambulate.
Ask the patient if they feel dizzy or light-headed.
Properly place the gait belt around the patient's waist and check the gait belt for tightness by slipping fingers between the gait belt and the patient.
Face the patient and place each of the feet in front of the patient's feet to prevent them from slipping.
Instruct the patient to push up on the bed on the count of three to assist with standing.
Count to three and assist the patient to a standing position.

steps.text: Move to weak side; hold belt
steps.rawText: Move to weak side; hold belt
steps.detailedTagText: Move to the weak side of the patient, slightly behind them. Hold the gait belt with palms and fingertips pointing upwards.
Assist the patient in a standing position.
Walk slightly behind the patient for a distance of 10 feet while safely holding the transfer belt.

steps.text: Assist back to bed; remove belt
steps.rawText: Assist back to bed; remove belt
steps.detailedTagText: Safely assist the client back to bed and remove the transfer belt.

steps.text: Call light; bed low and locked
steps.rawText: Call light; bed low and locked
steps.detailedTagText: Place the call light (or signaling device) within reach of the patient. Lower the bed to its lowest position and verify the bed brakes are locked.
Perform hand hygiene using the six-step technique for a minimum of 20 seconds with soap and water, or use an alcohol-based hand rub when hands are visibly clean.

steps.detailedTagText: Advise patients to report pain during movement.
Place one hand under the patient's elbow with palm facing up.
Place the other hand under the patient's wrist with palm facing up.
Watch the patient for objective signs of pain during movement.
Move their arms gently and stop if there is any resistance.
While keeping the patient's arm straight, raise their arm up and over their head (i.e., flexion).
Bring the patient's arm back down to their side (i.e., extension).
Complete flexion and extension movements of the shoulder according to the order in the restorative care plan.
Continue to support the elbow and wrist of the patient.
Keeping the patient's arm straight, move their entire arm out away from the body (i.e., abduction).
Move their arms gently and stop if there is any resistance.
Return the patient's arm to their side (adduction).
Lower the bed to its lowest position and verify the bed brakes are locked.
steps.note: Two actions, one step: lower AND lock. Physically press the brake and confirm resistance — evaluator watches for the physical check REGIONAL-TESTING-CENTER.pdf. S03 transcript: "make sure the bed is in low position" before washing ALL-TRANSCRIPTS-S01-S22.md.

steps.detailedTagText: Advise patients to report pain during movement.
Abduction/Adduction for Hip:
subSteps: Keeping the patient's leg straight, gently move their entire leg away from their
body (i.e., abduction).
Move their legs gently and stop if there is any resistance.
Ask the patient if they are experiencing any pain during movement.
Stop the ROM movement if the patient reports pain or displays objective signs
of pain.
Keeping the patient's leg straight, move their entire leg toward their body (i.e.,
adduction).
Complete abduction and adduction movements of the hip according to the
order in their restorative care plan.
Continue to correctly support joints by keeping one hand under the patient's
knee and the other hand under the patient's ankle.

steps.detailedTagText: Flexion/Extension of Knee and Hip:
subSteps: Bend the patient's knee and hip up toward the patient's trunk (i.e., flexion of
hip and knee at the same time).
Move the patient's leg gently and stop if there is any resistance.
Ask the patient if they are experiencing any pain during movement.
Stop the ROM movement if the patient reports pain or displays objective signs
of pain.
Straighten their knee and hip (i.e., extension of knee and hip at the same time).
Complete flexion and extension movements of the knee and hip according to
the order in the restorative care plan.

steps.detailedTagText: Introduce yourself by name and title, explain the procedure to the patient in clear, plain language, and obtain verbal consent before proceeding.

steps.detailedTagText: Provide for patient privacy (close the curtain, door, or privacy screen).

steps.detailedTagText: Lower the head of the bed.

steps.detailedTagText: Don clean, non-sterile gloves.

Remove gloves by turning them inside out — grasp the outside of one glove at the wrist and peel it off, then slide the fingers of the ungloved hand under the remaining glove at the wrist and peel it off turning inside out. Dispose of both gloves in the appropriate waste container without touching the outer surface. steps.detailedTagText: Raise the head of the bed to a comfortable level.

steps.detailedTagText: Wait nearby allowing for patient privacy.

steps.detailedTagText: Gently remove the bed pan.
Discard the soiled linen in the designated laundry hamper.
Assist with perineal care.
Empty the bedpan into the toilet.
Rinse the equipment used and empty the rinse water into the toilet.

skills.studentFocus: Hold catheter at urethra; strokes away; tubing downhill; never pull.

Raise the bed height to a working height.
Expose only the urethra and catheter.
Follow the tubing from the patient toward the drainage bag, ensuring that the tubing is at a lower level as it goes toward the bag. Be sure no kinks or elevation can cause backflow to the bladder.
Turn the patient or raise their hips and place a barrier (e.g., a towel, waterproof soaker pad, or disposable pad) under their buttocks.
Use the first washcloth with soap and water to carefully wash around the catheter where it exits the urethra.
Hold the catheter where it exits the urethra with one hand.
While holding the catheter, clean 4 inches down the catheter tube.
Clean with strokes moving away from the urethra.

steps.text: Use clean washcloth portion each stroke
steps.rawText: Use clean washcloth portion each stroke
steps.detailedTagText: Use a clean portion of washcloth for each stroke.

steps.text: Put first washcloth in linen bag
steps.rawText: Put first washcloth in linen bag
steps.detailedTagText: Put the soiled first washcloth in the linen bag.
Wet the second washcloth and rinse, using strokes only away from the urethra while continuing to hold the catheter where it exits the urethra.
Rinse using a clean portion of washcloth for each stroke.

steps.text: Put second washcloth in linen bag
steps.rawText: Put second washcloth in linen bag
steps.detailedTagText: Put the soiled second washcloth in the linen bag.
Pat dry with a towel.
Do not allow the tube to be pulled at any time during the procedure.
Replace the gown over the patient's perineal area.
While wearing gloves, empty the basin.
Rinse the basin.
Dry the basin.
Return the equipment to storage.
Dispose of soiled linen in a designated laundry hamper.
